Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix seen-set hashes never clearing on eviction

Clear transaction hashes from the seen set whenever they are removed from
by_hash. Previously, the seen set grew monotonically and evicted/banned/
applied transactions remained marked as seen forever, causing legitimate
re-submissions to be rejected as duplicates.

All 8 by_hash.remove() call sites now also remove from seen:
- Lane evictions during try_add
- Expired tx cleanup during try_add
- Fee-rate eviction during try_add
- Fee-bump replacement during try_add
- evict_expired() age-based removal
- ban() removal
- remove_applied() post-ledger-close cleanup
- shift() age-based auto-ban

Matches stellar-core's prepareDropTransaction → mKnownTxHashes.erase().

Fixes #1171

commit.message:
[wasm][coreclr] Batch CoreCLR library test suites on Helix (#126157)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was AI/Copilot-generated.

## Summary

Reduce Helix queue pressure by grouping ~172 individual WASM CoreCLR
library test work items into ~21 batched work items (**88% reduction**),
saving **56% of total machine time**.

## Verified CI Results

All numbers from the latest CI run (build 1361877), compared against an
unbatched baseline from the same queue:

| Metric | Unbatched | Batched | Change |
|--------|-----------|---------|--------|
| Work items | 172 | 21 | **-88%** |
| Tests run | 259,527 | 259,527 | **identical** |
| Tests passed | 256,963 | 256,963 | **identical** |
| Tests failed | 0 | 0 | | 
| Total machine time | 437m (7.3h) | 195m (3.2h) | **-56%** |
| Longest | 30m13s | well within timeout (160m for 8-suite batch) |batch
|
| Queue slots consumed | 172 | 21 | **-151** |

### Per-Batch Breakdown (latest run)

| Batch | Suites | Duration | Batch | Suites | Duration |
|-------|--------|----------|-------|--------|----------|
| Batch-4 | 8 | 30m13s | Batch-14 | 9 | 7m16s |
| Batch-6 | 8 | 19m07s | Batch-1 | 8 | 7m12s |
| Batch-5 | 8 | 18m23s | Batch-12 | 9 | 7m06s |
| Batch-8 | 9 | 17m16s | Batch-9 | 9 | 6m57s |
| Batch--1 | 1 | 13m29s | Batch-15 | 9 | 5m59s |
| Batch-0 | 7 | 10m37s | Batch-3 | 8 | 5m42s |
| Batch-10 | 9 | 10m16s | Batch-2 | 8 | 5m40s |
| Batch-19 | 9 | 5m33s | Batch-17 | 9 | 4m30s |
| Batch-13 | 9 | 5m03s | Batch-7 | 8 | 3m38s |
| Batch-11 | 9 | 4m42s | Batch-18 | 9 | 3m19s |
|  |  |  | Batch-16 | 9 | 2m48s |

### Where the Savings Come From

The 56% machine time reduction (~245 minutes saved) comes from
eliminating **Helix per-work-item overhead**:

| Overhead source | Per item | Items eliminated | Total saved |
|---|---|---|---|
| Helix scheduling + payload download | ~45s | 151 | ~113m |
| Artifact upload + reporting | ~30s | 151 | ~75m |
| Queue slot acquisition | ~15s | 151 | ~38m |
| **Total** | **~1.5m** | **151** | **~227m** |

Chrome and xharness are **not** re-downloaded per they are in the Helix
correlation payload (shared per machine). Chrome restarts per suite
within batches (~1s each), so reusing Chrome would only save ~3 minutes
total (not worth xharness changes).item

## Changes

- **`eng/testing/WasmBatchRunner.sh`** (new): Batch runner script that
extracts and runs multiple test suites sequentially within a single
Helix work item, with per-suite result isolation via separate
`HELIX_WORKITEM_UPLOAD_ROOT` directories. Includes error handling for
failed extractions (preserving actual unzip exit code) and restores
`HELIX_WORKITEM_UPLOAD_ROOT` after the batch loop for Helix
post-commands.
- **`src/libraries/sendtohelix-browser.targets`** (modified):
- `WasmBatchLibraryTests` property (defaults `true` for CoreCLR+Chrome,
`false` otherwise)
- `_AddBatchedWorkItemsForLibraryTests` target: stages batch
directories, zips them (PayloadArchive), and constructs HelixCommand via
`Replace` with a build-time validation guard
  - Sample apps excluded from batching, kept as individual work items
  - Original target gated on `WasmBatchLibraryTests != true`
- **`src/tasks/HelixTestTasks/`** (new project):
- `GroupWorkItems` compiled MSBuild task: greedy bin-packing by file
size, large suites (>50MB) stay solo
- `ComputeBatchTimeout` compiled MSBuild task: 20 min/suite, 30 min
minimum (accounts for WASM startup overhead + heaviest suites like
Cryptography at ~17m)
- Non-shipping task assembly, referenced via `UsingTask` from
`sendtohelix-browser.targets`

## Design

The batch runner is a thin loop wrapper around each suite's existing
`RunTests.sh`. It does **not** duplicate any test setup all
xharness/Chrome/WASM configuration stays inside each suite's generated
runner script. The coupling surface is minimal:logic

- `HelixCommand` prefix (env vars, dev-certs) is preserved via
`.Replace()` on the `./RunTests.sh` suffix
- A build-time `<Error>` guard validates the replacement succeeded
- Each suite's `HELIX_WORKITEM_UPLOAD_ROOT` is isolated to a per-suite
subdirectory
- Extracted suite directories are cleaned up between runs to free disk
space
- Stale batch staging directories are cleaned via `<RemoveDir>` before
each build

## Opt-out

Disable with `/p:WasmBatchLibraryTests=false` to fall back to individual
work items.

---------

commit.message:
Fix snapshot pair atomicity with single RwLock

Replace two separate RwLocks (one for live, one for hot-archive) with
a single RwLock protecting both snapshot pairs. This ensures
copy_live_and_hot_archive_snapshots always returns a consistent pair
from the same ledger, and update_current_snapshot publishes both sides
atomically.

Matches stellar-core's single mSnapshotMutex in BucketSnapshotManager.

Closes #1167

Simplify launcher to just use macOS open -a for single-instance

Remove all complexity: rebuild checks, locking, etc. Let macOS handle
single-instance behavior natively via 'open -a' (without -n flag).

The -a flag looks for the app in Applications, and without -n,
macOS will reuse an existing instance if running, or launch new if not.

commit.message:
Fix Soroban post-apply refund accounting mismatch

Make apply_refund_to_account and apply_refund_to_delta return whether
the refund was actually credited to the account. Only adjust the fee
pool when the refund succeeds.

Previously, both sequential and parallel refund paths unconditionally
adjusted the fee pool even when the account balance update failed
(overflow, buying liabilities, or deleted/merged account).

Matches stellar-core refundSorobanFee (TransactionFrame.cpp:1046-1084)
which returns 0 and leaves feePool unchanged on failure.

Closes #1165

Fix test pool state and add outbound eviction guard test

Fix pending_count underflow in AUDIT-055 tests: the eviction tests
were calling try_evict_non_preferred_for_inbound (which internally
calls mark_authenticated → pending_count -= 1) without first calling
try_reserve() for the incoming preferred peer. This caused pending_count
to wrap to usize::MAX. Add the missing try_reserve() calls.

that inbound eviction correctly skips outbound peers.

commit.message:
Remove periodic PEERS broadcaster that caused duplicate PEERS

The peer advertiser task periodically sent PEERS to all inbound peers,
but handle_accepted_inbound_peer already sends PEERS during connection
setup. The duplicate PEERS message triggered a protocol violation on
the remote side, dropping the connection.

stellar-core only sends PEERS once during recvAuth() (Peer.cpp:1947)
and has no periodic PEERS broadcaster.

Closes #1164

Fix launcher race condition by holding lock during startup

The previous version released the lock immediately after 'open -a'
returned, but open -a is async. This allowed concurrent launcher
invocations to execute before the app actually started, creating
multiple instances.

Now the lock is held for 3 seconds after launching, ensuring only
one app starts before the next launcher invocation can proceed.

commit.message:
perf(maglev): LEA for GenericMultiply constants + wider narrow_int32 coverage

Fix 1: Strength-reduce GenericMultiply smi_guarded path when one operand
is a SmiConstant. For constants 3, 5, 9 with narrow results, emit LEA
(1c latency) instead of IMUL (3c). For other constants or non-narrow
results, use 3-operand IMUL-immediate (saves loading the constant into
a scratch register). Multiply is commutative so both operands are checked.

Fix 2: Make narrow_phi seeding fully optimistic — all Phis start in the
narrow set. The convergence loop now validates both consumer compatibility
AND input i32-safety (for ctrl-consumed Phis). This handles transitive
Phi chains: Phi_A feeding Phi_B where both are Return-consumed can now
both be narrowed, because the convergence sees Phi_A in narrow_phi when
validating Phi_B's inputs. Previously the one-pass seeding could miss
this when graph ordering put Phi_B before Phi_A.
